items.name: Add TOML and UUID dependencies
items.description: Add github.com/BurntSushi/toml for .forgectl/config parsing and github.com/google/uuid for session_id generation to go.mod.
steps: cd forgectl && go get github.com/BurntSushi/toml
cd forgectl && go get github.com/google/uuid

items.name: Config types and TOML parsing with defaults
items.description: Define all Config structs (Config, SpecifyingConfig, PlanningConfig, etc.), implement DefaultConfig(), LoadConfig(), and ValidateConfig() with constraint checking per the spec.
steps: Define all Config struct types with toml and json tags
Implement DefaultConfig() returning all spec-defined defaults
Implement LoadConfig(projectRoot string) that decodes TOML over defaults
Implement ValidateConfig(cfg Config) that checks all per-phase constraints (batch>=1, min_rounds>=1, min<=max for each eval section, logs.retention_days>=0, logs.max_files>=0)
Return []string of violations so caller can print and exit 1

tests.description: DefaultConfig returns all spec-defined defaults (specifying.batch=3, implementing.batch=2, etc.)
LoadConfig merges TOML overrides onto defaults — only explicitly set fields change
LoadConfig with partial config (only [specifying]) leaves other sections at defaults
ValidateConfig returns error when specifying.eval.min_rounds > max_rounds
ValidateConfig returns error when implementing.batch < 1
ValidateConfig returns error when logs.retention_days < 0

items.name: Project root discovery via .forgectl/ walk
items.description: Implement FindProjectRoot() that walks up the directory hierarchy until .forgectl/ is found, identical to how git discovers .git/.
steps: Implement FindProjectRoot() that starts at os.Getwd() and walks up
Return (projectRoot string, err error) — error if filesystem root reached without finding .forgectl/
Error message: "No .forgectl directory found."
Export function for use by all cmd/ handlers

tests.description: FindProjectRoot finds .forgectl/ in current directory
FindProjectRoot finds .forgectl/ two levels up from current directory
FindProjectRoot returns error with correct message when no .forgectl/ exists in hierarchy

items.name: Update ForgeState schema with config, session_id, and new specifying fields
items.description: Update ForgeState to add Config and SessionID fields; remove top-level BatchSize/MinRounds/MaxRounds/UserGuided; rename current_spec→current_specs; add Domains and CrossReference to SpecifyingState; add SpecCommits to PlanQueueEntry and ActivePlan; remove legacy CommitHash from CompletedSpec.
steps: Add Config Config and SessionID string to ForgeState
Remove BatchSize, MinRounds, MaxRounds, UserGuided from ForgeState
Rename CurrentSpec *ActiveSpec to CurrentSpecs []*ActiveSpec in SpecifyingState
Add Domains map[string]DomainMeta to SpecifyingState (omitempty)
Add CrossReference map[string]*CrossReferenceState to SpecifyingState (omitempty)
Define DomainMeta struct with CodeSearchRoots []string
Define CrossReferenceState struct with Domain, Round, Evals fields
Add SpecCommits []string to PlanQueueEntry
Remove Topic string from PlanQueueEntry
Add SpecCommits []string to ActivePlan
Remove CommitHash string (legacy) from CompletedSpec — use CommitHashes only
Add DomainPath string to CompletedSpec and ActiveSpec
Add CROSS_REFERENCE, CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL, CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW state constants

tests.description: ForgeState JSON round-trips with config and session_id fields
SpecifyingState JSON round-trips with current_specs array (null and populated)
PlanQueueEntry validates with spec_commits field present and topic field absent

items.name: Update state Save/Load/Recover to use configurable state_dir from project root
items.description: Remove --dir flag from root command. All commands call FindProjectRoot() + load config to derive the state dir. Add StateDir() helper. Update Exists/Recover/Load/Save to accept the resolved absolute state dir path.
steps: Remove stateDir string var and --dir flag from cmd/root.go
Add StateDir(projectRoot string, cfg Config) string helper that resolves relative or absolute state_dir
Update all cmd/ handlers (init, advance, status, eval, add-commit, reconcile-commit) to call FindProjectRoot(), LoadConfig(), StateDir() at the start
Ensure os.MkdirAll is called on state dir before first Save
Recover/Load/Save/Exists signatures remain (dir string) — caller now passes the resolved absolute path

tests.description: StateDir returns absolute path when state_dir is relative (joined to project root)
StateDir returns state_dir unchanged when it is already absolute
State file is created at <project_root>/<state_dir>/forgectl-state.json after init
Any command resolves state dir from .forgectl/ hierarchy walk even when run from a subdirectory

items.name: Session archiving to state_dir/sessions/
items.description: Implement archiving of completed sessions to <state_dir>/sessions/<domain>-<date>.json. Archive the active forgectl-state.json when session reaches terminal state. Archived files are git-tracked; active state is gitignored.
steps: Implement ArchiveSession(stateDir string, domain string, s *ForgeState) error that copies state to sessions/<domain>-<YYYY-MM-DD>.json
Call ArchiveSession at the appropriate terminal state transition (DONE in implementing, or PHASE_SHIFT from COMPLETE in specifying when session started at specifying)
os.MkdirAll sessions/ subdirectory before writing

tests.description: ArchiveSession creates sessions/<domain>-<date>.json in state_dir
Archived file contains the full ForgeState JSON
Archiving at implementing DONE creates sessions file in configured state_dir

items.name: Init command overhaul: remove old flags, wire config, generate session_id
items.description: Remove --batch-size/--min-rounds/--max-rounds/--guided/--no-guided flags. Add project root discovery, TOML config read+validate, session_id UUID generation, config locking into state. Update plan queue validation to require spec_commits and reject topic.
steps: Remove initBatchSize, initMinRounds, initMaxRounds, initGuided, initNoGuided vars and flag registrations
In runInit: call FindProjectRoot(), LoadConfig(), ValidateConfig() — exit 1 on any failure
Generate session_id via uuid.New().String()
Set s.Config = cfg and s.SessionID = sessionID on ForgeState
Remove top-level BatchSize/MinRounds/MaxRounds/UserGuided from state construction
Pass resolved state dir from StateDir() to state.Save()
Update ValidatePlanQueue to reject 'topic' field and require 'spec_commits' array
Update PlanQueueSchema() string for updated error output
Update NewPlanningState to populate SpecCommits on ActivePlan

tests.description: Init creates state at .forgectl/state/ with config object and session_id populated
Init applies config defaults when .forgectl/config has only partial sections
Init locks config into state file — config object matches .forgectl/config values
Init exits 1 when .forgectl/ directory not found in hierarchy
Init exits 1 when .forgectl/config has invalid TOML
Init exits 1 when .forgectl/config has constraint violation (min_rounds > max_rounds)
Init exits 1 when plan queue contains 'topic' field (removed from schema)
Init at planning phase stores spec_commits from plan queue entry in ActivePlan
Init when run from subdirectory finds .forgectl/ in ancestor and writes state to correct absolute path

items.name: Spec lifecycle batch processing and updated state machine
items.description: Update specifying phase to process batches of specs (domain-grouped, up to specifying.batch). Update ORIENT to select batch into current_specs. Update SELECT/DRAFT/EVALUATE/REFINE/ACCEPT output and transitions to operate on batches. Gate --message on enable_commits. Remove --file from DRAFT.
steps: Update ORIENT: select next domain's batch from queue (up to config.Specifying.Batch), set s.Specifying.CurrentSpecs
Update SELECT output: show all specs in batch with file, topic, sources
Update DRAFT: remove --file flag handling; show batch of spec files to draft; mention add-queue-item for new discoveries
Update EVALUATE: batch eval file convention <domain>/specs/.eval/batch-<N>-r<M>.md; gate --message on enable_commits
Update REFINE output to show batch of spec files
Update ACCEPT: move all current_specs to completed; check if domain has more queued specs — if yes ORIENT, if no CROSS_REFERENCE
Use config.Specifying.Eval.MinRounds and MaxRounds (from state s.Config) instead of s.MinRounds/s.MaxRounds
Update output.go specifying output functions to accept/display batch context

tests.description: ORIENT selects up to specifying.batch specs from the same domain
ORIENT selects only specs from the next pending domain (no cross-domain batches)
ACCEPT with more specs queued in same domain transitions to ORIENT
ACCEPT with domain exhausted transitions to CROSS_REFERENCE
EVALUATE PASS with enable_commits=false does not require --message
EVALUATE PASS with enable_commits=true and missing --message returns error
Domain with fewer specs than batch size produces one batch of smaller size

items.name: CROSS_REFERENCE, CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL, CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW state machine
items.description: Implement the three cross-reference states triggered after each domain completes. Uses config.specifying.cross_reference for min/max rounds and user_review. CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW always fires once after first passing eval. After review, advance to next domain or DONE.
steps: Add CROSS_REFERENCE, CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL, CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW to StateNames in types.go (already done in state.schema item)
Implement CROSS_REFERENCE transition: initialize CrossReferenceState for domain, transition to CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL

Implement CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL: require --verdict and --eval-report; use config.Specifying.CrossReference min/max rounds; PASS>=min and round==1 → CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW; PASS>=min and round>1 → next domain or DONE; PASS<min → CROSS_REFERENCE; FAIL<max → CROSS_REFERENCE; FAIL>=max (forced) → same as PASS>=min
Implement CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW: if queue non-empty (ANY domain, not just current domain), advance to ORIENT; if queue empty, advance to next pending domain or DONE
Implement output for CROSS_REFERENCE: list session specs + existing specs in domain directory
Implement output for CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL: show round, agent count from config.Specifying.CrossReference
Implement output for CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW: show user_review-gated STOP message; show add-queue-item and set-roots hints

tests.description: CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL PASS at round 1 with min_rounds=1 transitions to CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW
CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL PASS below min_rounds loops back to CROSS_REFERENCE
CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL PASS at round>1 with min_rounds met skips review, goes to next domain or DONE
CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL FAIL below max_rounds loops back to CROSS_REFERENCE
CROSS_REFERENCE_EVAL FAIL at max_rounds (forced) on round 1 enters CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW
CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW with empty queue advances to next domain ORIENT or DONE
CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW output shows STOP when user_review=true
CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW output shows 'Domain cross-reference complete' when user_review=false
After last domain's CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW, advancing transitions to DONE

items.name: add-queue-item and set-roots commands
items.description: Implement add-queue-item (appends spec to specifying queue, valid in DRAFT/CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW/DONE/RECONCILE_REVIEW) and set-roots (stores domain code search roots in specifying.domains, valid in CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW/DONE).
steps: Implement addqueueitem.go: load state, validate phase==specifying and state in allowed set, infer domain if not at DONE, append SpecQueueEntry to s.Specifying.Queue, save state, print confirmation
Validate --file exists on disk; error: "file <path> does not exist. add-queue-item registers specs that have already been written. Create the spec file first, then register it."
Validate --name is unique across queue and completed specs; error if duplicate
Derive domain_path from --file (two levels up from spec file, e.g. optimizer/specs/foo.md -> domain_path = optimizer/)

Implement setroots.go: load state, validate phase==specifying and state in (CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW, DONE), infer domain if not at DONE, set s.Specifying.Domains[domain]=DomainMeta{CodeSearchRoots: args}, save state, print confirmation
Validate the domain has at least one completed spec before accepting set-roots; error if not
Reject set-roots if no positional path arguments provided
Reject add-queue-item outside specifying phase with error: "add-queue-item is only valid in the specifying phase (current phase: <phase>)."
Reject add-queue-item outside valid states with error naming current state
Reject set-roots outside specifying phase with error: "set-roots is only valid in the specifying phase (current phase: <phase>)."
Reject set-roots outside valid states with error naming current state
At DONE, --domain flag is required for both commands; elsewhere domain is inferred from current batch

tests.description: add-queue-item at DRAFT appends spec to queue with domain inferred from current batch
add-queue-item at DONE with --domain appends spec to queue
set-roots at CROSS_REFERENCE_REVIEW stores roots in specifying.domains for current domain
set-roots at DONE with --domain stores roots in specifying.domains
add-queue-item with --file pointing to non-existent path returns error
add-queue-item with --name that duplicates existing queue/completed entry returns error
add-queue-item outside specifying phase returns phase-level error
add-queue-item at EVALUATE returns error naming current state
set-roots for domain with no completed specs returns error
set-roots with no path arguments returns error
set-roots outside specifying phase returns phase-level error
set-roots at DRAFT returns error naming current state
add-queue-item at DONE without --domain returns error

items.name: Spec reconciliation enable_commits gating and user_review output
items.description: Gate --message on enable_commits in RECONCILE_EVAL. Update RECONCILE_REVIEW output to show 'STOP' when user_review=true vs 'Reconciliation review complete' when false. add-queue-item available at RECONCILE_REVIEW.
steps: In RECONCILE_EVAL PASS handling: only require --message when s.Config.General.EnableCommits is true
In RECONCILE_REVIEW output: check s.Config.Specifying.Reconciliation.UserReview to select action text
Ensure add-queue-item command accepts RECONCILE_REVIEW as a valid state

tests.description: RECONCILE_EVAL PASS with enable_commits=false does not require --message
RECONCILE_EVAL PASS with enable_commits=true and no --message returns error
RECONCILE_REVIEW output includes STOP when user_review=true
RECONCILE_REVIEW output says 'Reconciliation review complete' when user_review=false

items.name: Auto-generate plan queue at specifying→planning phase shift
items.description: At PHASE_SHIFT (specifying→planning), make --from optional. When omitted, auto-generate plan queue from completed specs grouped by domain using commit hashes and set-roots data. Override mode when --from provided.
steps: In advancePhaseShift for specifying→planning: check if --from provided

If --from omitted: auto-generate plan queue — group completed specs by domain, for each domain build PlanQueueEntry with name='<Domain> Implementation Plan', file='<domain>/.forge_workspace/implementation_plan/plan.json', specs=all domain spec files, spec_commits=deduplicated commit_hashes across domain, code_search_roots from s.Specifying.Domains[domain] or default ['<domain>/']
Validate auto-generated queue (same ValidatePlanQueue checks)
If --from provided: validate external file, use as override (existing behavior)
Update PHASE_SHIFT output to show both auto-generate and --from options
Update PHASE_SHIFT output to display domains, spec count, and roots summary

tests.description: Advance from PHASE_SHIFT without --from auto-generates plan queue grouped by domain
Auto-generated plan entry uses code_search_roots from set-roots when configured
Auto-generated plan entry defaults code_search_roots to ['<domain>/'] when set-roots not called
Auto-generated spec_commits deduplicates commit hashes across all specs in domain
Advance from PHASE_SHIFT with --from uses external plan queue (override mode)
Advance from PHASE_SHIFT with --from pointing to invalid plan queue prints errors and stays at PHASE_SHIFT

items.name: enable_commits gating in planning and implementing, and .forge_workspace paths
items.description: Gate --message on enable_commits in ACCEPT (planning), IMPLEMENT first round, and COMMIT (implementing). Update implementing to use config.implementing.batch. Update all output paths from .workspace/ to .forge_workspace/.
steps: In advancePlanning ACCEPT: only require --message when s.Config.General.EnableCommits is true
In advanceImplementing IMPLEMENT: only require --message on first round when s.Config.General.EnableCommits is true
In advanceImplementing COMMIT: only require --message when s.Config.General.EnableCommits is true
Replace s.BatchSize references in advanceImplementing with s.Config.Implementing.Batch
Replace s.MinRounds/s.MaxRounds references with s.Config.Implementing.Eval.MinRounds/MaxRounds in implementing
Replace s.MinRounds/s.MaxRounds references with s.Config.Planning.Eval.MinRounds/MaxRounds in planning
Update all output.go path strings from .workspace/ to .forge_workspace/
Update output text to 'After completion of the above, advance to continue.' pattern per spec diffs

tests.description: IMPLEMENT first round with enable_commits=false advances without --message
IMPLEMENT first round with enable_commits=true and no --message returns error
COMMIT with enable_commits=false advances without --message
Implementing batch selection uses config.Implementing.Batch (not top-level BatchSize)
Planning ACCEPT with enable_commits=false does not require --message

items.name: Activity logging to ~/.forgectl/logs/ in JSONL format
items.description: Implement Logger struct, log file creation at init, log entry appending for advance/add-commit/reconcile-commit, and log pruning at init. Best-effort: failures print warning to stderr, never fail commands.
steps: Create state/logger.go: define Logger struct with enabled bool and path string
Implement NewLogger(cfg LogsConfig, phase string, sessionID string) *Logger — derives log file path
Implement Logger.Write(entry LogEntry) — appends JSON line to file; on any error, prints warning to stderr and returns
Implement PruneLogs(cfg LogsConfig) — age-based then count-based deletion in ~/.forgectl/logs/
Define LogEntry struct with ts, cmd, phase, prev_state (omitempty), state, detail fields
In cmd/init.go: after state.Save, if cfg.Logs.Enabled, call PruneLogs then NewLogger then Write init entry
In cmd/advance.go: after state.Save, write advance log entry with prev_state and detail
In cmd/addcommit.go: after state.Save, write add-commit log entry
In cmd/reconcilecommit.go: after state.Save, write reconcile-commit log entry
status, eval, validate, add-queue-item, set-roots do NOT write log entries

tests.description: Init creates log file at ~/.forgectl/logs/<phase>-<session_id[:8]>.jsonl with init entry
Advance appends log entry with prev_state and state fields
add-commit appends log entry with spec_id, spec_name, hash in detail
reconcile-commit appends log entry with hash and matched_specs in detail
Pruning at init deletes log files older than retention_days
Pruning at init deletes oldest files when count exceeds max_files
logs.enabled=false: no log file created, no pruning, no warnings
Log write failure (read-only dir) prints warning to stderr and command exits 0
Both retention_days and max_files apply: age-based runs first, then count-based on remainder

items.name: validate command with auto-detection and --type override
items.description: Implement `forgectl validate [--type spec-queue|plan-queue|plan] <file_path>`. Auto-detects JSON type from top-level key. Reuses existing validation functions. No session required. Exit 0 on success, 1 on any error.
steps: Create cmd/validate.go with Cobra command 'validate'
Register --type flag (optional): spec-queue, plan-queue, plan
Accept one positional arg: file_path
Read and JSON-parse the file; error on invalid JSON
If --type not set: inspect top-level key (specs→spec-queue, plans→plan-queue, context→plan); error with hint if unrecognized
If --type set: verify top-level key matches expected; error with 'did you mean' hint if mismatch
Print 'Detected: <type> (top-level key: "<key>")' for auto-detect
Call appropriate ValidateXxx function
On success: print 'Validated: <filename> — N entries, no errors.' (use item count for plan, entry count for queues)
On failure: print field-level errors with 1-indexed numbering
Do not call FindProjectRoot() or load state — standalone command
Do not write log entries

tests.description: validate auto-detects spec-queue from top-level 'specs' key and reports success
validate auto-detects plan-queue from top-level 'plans' key and reports success
validate auto-detects plan from top-level 'context' key and reports success
validate --type plan-queue validates plan queue with spec_commits field
validate with unrecognized top-level key prints auto-detection failure with hint
validate --type spec-queue with file that has top-level 'plans' key prints type mismatch error
validate with invalid JSON prints parse error with line/column
validate with validation errors prints numbered field-level error list
validate plan.json resolves ref paths relative to the file's directory

items.name: Status compact output with progress line and --verbose flag
items.description: Update status to compact format with one-line progress summary. Add --verbose/-v flag that appends full session overview. Update session header to show .forgectl/state/ path. Update config display to use config object values.
steps: Add --verbose / -v flag to statusCmd
Update PrintStatus to accept verbose bool parameter
Compact output: session path as .forgectl/state/forgectl-state.json, phase, config from s.Config (batch=N, rounds=min-max, guided=bool), current state action, Progress line
Progress lines: specifying='Progress: N/T specs completed, Q queued', planning='Progress: round N of M', implementing='Progress: N passed, F failed, R remaining'
Verbose mode appends: --- Specifying --- section with completed specs+evals (if reached), --- Planning --- section with evals (if reached), --- Implementing --- section with per-layer per-item status
Update config display: use s.Config.General.UserGuided, phase-appropriate batch and round values from s.Config
Update 'Started at implementing directly' output to show '(started here)' and correct config display

tests.description: status compact output shows session path, phase, config from config object, and Progress line
status --verbose appends full session overview sections for each phase reached
status -v short form works identically to --verbose
status without --verbose does not print full session overview
Config display uses config.implementing.batch when in implementing phase

items.name: Tests for all new behavior
items.description: Write comprehensive tests covering project root discovery, TOML config, init overhaul, CROSS_REFERENCE state machine, add-queue-item/set-roots, auto-generate plan queue, enable_commits gating, activity logging, validate command, and status verbose flag.
steps: Add config tests to state/config_test.go: DefaultConfig values, LoadConfig partial override, ValidateConfig constraint violations
Add root discovery tests to state/root_test.go: found in cwd, found 2 levels up, not found

Update state/advance_test.go: update existing tests to remove BatchSize/MinRounds/MaxRounds from ForgeState construction; add config object; add CROSS_REFERENCE state machine tests; add enable_commits gating tests; add auto-generate plan queue tests
Create state/logger_test.go: log file creation, entry appending, pruning by age, pruning by count, disabled logging, best-effort failure
Update cmd/commands_test.go: update init tests to use .forgectl/ fixture and config file instead of old flags; add validate command tests
Create cmd/validate_test.go or add to commands_test.go: auto-detect, --type override, errors
Ensure go test ./... passes

tests.description: go test ./... passes with all new tests green
All existing advance_test.go tests updated to use Config object pass
CROSS_REFERENCE full state machine flow test passes
Activity logging integration test: init creates file, advance appends entry
Validate command tests cover all auto-detect and --type paths
